name: Nu'uanu Pali Lookout
tagline: The most dramatic viewpoint on Oahu — sheer cliffs, howling winds, and the entire Windward Coast spread out below.
bestTime: Morning for clearest views. Afternoon clouds roll in. Trade winds blast through here — hold onto hats, phones, and small children
kamaaina: $3 parking for Hawaii residents with valid ID
talkStory: This is where King Kamehameha I won the decisive 1795 Battle of Nu'uanu, driving hundreds of O'ahu warriors over the 1,000-foot cliffs to unify the Hawaiian Islands. The views are jaw-dropping — the entire Windward Coast from Kaneohe to Kailua laid out below the Ko'olau cliffs. The wind here is legendary — sustained 30-40 mph gusts are normal, and 60+ mph days happen. It's a quick stop (15-20 min) right off the Pali Highway between Honolulu and Kailua. Great combined with Old Pali Road hike nearby.
